name: Hot Maryland Crab Dip
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons apple brandy or white wine (optional)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
2. If the crab meat is frozen, drain it well in a sieve, pushing the solids with paper towel to remove all the moisture. Fresh or frozen, pick over it to remove any bits of cartilage and set aside.
3. In a food processor, puree cottage cheese, cream cheese, horseradish, mustard, milk, lemon juice for 3 to 4 minutes.
4. In a small bowl, combine the cottage cheese mixture (which should be very smooth), crab meat, onion, and celery and stir until well blended.
5. Spoon the mixture into an oven-proof dish and bake for 25 to 30 minutes, until bubbly and lightly golden on top.
6. Sprinkle with cayenne or paprika and parsley, for garnish. Serve hot with crisp bread.
7. Variation: When thinned with 1/4 cup nonfat milk, this dip becomes enough sauce for 4 servings of pasta.
